channel.title: Sample Tech Feed
channel.description: Sample RSS feed for newsletter-factory testing

item.title: New Open-Source LLM Beats GPT-4 on Coding Benchmarks
item.description: A research team released a new 70B parameter model fine-tuned on code that outperforms GPT-4 on HumanEval and MBPP benchmarks while running on a single A100 GPU. The model weights and training code are available on HuggingFace under Apache 2.0.

item.title: Anthropic Releases Claude's Extended Thinking API
item.description: Anthropic launched an extended thinking mode for Claude that surfaces the model's chain-of-thought reasoning as a structured output. Developers can now access thinking tokens directly, enabling new debugging and transparency workflows for AI applications.

item.title: Bun 2.0 Released with Native TypeScript Decorators and 40% Faster Bundler
item.description: Bun 2.0 ships with native TypeScript decorator support, a 40% faster bundler, built-in test coverage reporting, and a new package manager lock file format. The upgrade path from 1.x is fully backwards-compatible.

item.title: Ask HN: What's Your Current Agentic AI Stack?
item.description: A popular Hacker News thread where developers share their current production stacks for agentic AI systems. Key themes: Claude + tool use, LangGraph for orchestration, and growing preference for deterministic pipelines over fully autonomous agents.

item.title: Postgres 18 Preview: Vectorized Query Execution
item.description: The Postgres 18 development branch now includes an experimental vectorized query execution engine that delivers 3-5x speedup on analytical queries. The feature is opt-in via a GUC setting and does not affect OLTP performance.

item.title: Vercel Acquires Tremor — React Component Library for Dashboards
item.description: Vercel acquired Tremor, the open-source React component library focused on analytics dashboards. The library will be integrated into the v0 component generation platform. Tremor remains open-source under MIT license.

item.title: Fly.io Launches Persistent Volumes with Point-in-Time Recovery
item.description: Fly.io added point-in-time recovery to persistent volumes, enabling teams to restore volume state to any second in the past 7 days. Pricing is $0.15/GB/month for the snapshot storage.

item.title: The Hidden Costs of Microservices at Small Scale
item.description: An engineer at a 15-person startup shares a post-mortem on their microservices migration: 6 months of velocity loss, 3x infrastructure costs, and eventually a return to a modular monolith. Includes specific numbers and a framework for deciding when microservices make sense.
